data.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T10] Infrastructure / IaC (overlay) — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (PASS, no findings).

T10 is an overlay that attaches only when the plan provisions or configures infrastructure — cloud resources, IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible), or Kubernetes/Helm. This epic's deliverables do not fall in that surface:
- `docs/workflow-engine.md` — documentation only.
- Eval specs, deterministic-scorer registry, `run_eval()` — application/library code under `src/rebar/llm/`, not infra.
- `eval-discipline` / `eval-live` — GitHub Actions CI workflow definitions (CI config), not provisioned cloud resources or IaC tooling. None of T10's binary checks (remote state+locking, least-privilege IAM, drift, prevent_destroy on stateful RDS/S3/volumes, secrets manager, autoscaling/quotas, IaC teardown) have a target here.

Per ANTI-FP ("not-applicable for non-infra tickets"), the overlay does not attach.

Affirmations (the CI-layer analogues of the cost/secrets/blast-radius concerns are already handled appropriately, even though T10 proper does not apply): `eval-live` is fork-safe (no live call on fork PRs or Dependabot), concurrency-capped, response-cached, non-blocking (continue-on-error), and provider spend-cap guidance is documented (AC7); `eval-discipline` runs with no model call / zero API cost on every PR (AC6). Empty findings list for T10.
